debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
primes : List ℕ
h_precond : findExponents_precond n primes
⊢ findExponents_postcond n primes (findExponents n primes h_precond) h_precond

failed_attempts:
/-- The theorem `findExponents_spec_satisfied` is FALSE as stated.
    Counterexample: n = 4, primes = [3].
    The precondition holds (4 > 0, [3].length > 0, 3 is prime, [3] has no duplicates),
    but findExponents 4 [3] = [(3, 0)] and the postcondition requires
    4 = foldl (· * ·^·) 1 [(3, 0)] = 1 * 3^0 = 1, which is false.
    The precondition is missing the requirement that all prime factors of n
    are contained in the primes list. -/
theorem findExponents_spec_is_false :
    ¬ ∀ (n : Nat) (primes : List Nat) (h : findExponents_precond n primes),
      findExponents_postcond n primes (findExponents n primes h) h := by
  intro h
  have := h 4 [3] ⟨by omega, by simp, by native_decide, by simp [List.Nodup]⟩
  simp [findExponents_postcond, findExponents, findExponents.countFactors,
        findExponents.countFactor, List.foldl] at this
-- !benchmark @end proof_aux


theorem findExponents_spec_satisfied (n: Nat) (primes: List Nat) (h_precond : findExponents_precond (n) (primes)) :
    findExponents_postcond (n) (primes) (findExponents (n) (primes) h_precond) h_precond := by
  -- !benchmark @start proof
  sorry
  -- !benchmark @end proof
